Applying the same reset inside each slash-command branch would be a small defensive improvement.

target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #50054: fix(acp): add distributed session locking with fail-closed redis fallback
github_context:

- Title: fix(acp): add distributed session locking with fail-closed redis fallback
- State: OPEN
- Author: Dnick20
- Labels: size: L, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: ЁЯзВ unranked krab, merge-risk: ЁЯЪи session-state, merge-risk: ЁЯЪи message-delivery, merge-risk: ЁЯЪи availability, status: ЁЯУг needs proof
- Changed file count available to wrapper: 5
- Changed files: .env.example, src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts, src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts, src/auto-reply/reply/session-lock-manager.test.ts, src/auto-reply/reply/session-lock-manager.ts

Body:
```markdown
## Summary
Adds distributed ACP session locking with Redis support and fail-closed behavior when Redis is configured but unavailable.

## What changed
- added `SessionLockManager` abstraction
- added `LocalSessionLockManager`
- added `RedisSessionLockManager`
- added `FailClosedSessionLockManager`
- wired lock acquire/release/renew into `tryDispatchAcpReply`
- skip ACP dispatch with `acp_execution_locked` when lock is contended or acquire fails
- documented:
  - `OPENCLAW_ACP_SESSION_LOCK_REDIS_URL`
  - `OPENCLAW_ACP_SESSION_LOCK_TTL_MS`
- added unit + dispatch integration tests

## Locking behavior
- no Redis configured тЖТ local in-process lock manager
- Redis configured and healthy тЖТ Redis lock manager
- Redis configured but init fails тЖТ fail closed, no fallback to local
- release only if owner matches
- renew only if owner matches
- renew is best-effort during long-running turns

## Validation
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm tsgo`
- `pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/session-lock-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts`
```

  - `LocalSessionLockManager`
  - `RedisSessionLockManager`
  - `FailClosedSessionLockManager`

  - `OPENCLAW_ACP_SESSION_LOCK_REDIS_URL`
  - `OPENCLAW_ACP_SESSION_LOCK_TTL_MS`
- adds unit + dispatch integration coverage

**Behavior:**
- no Redis configured тЖТ local in-process lock manager
- Redis configured and healthy тЖТ Redis lock manager
- Redis configured but init fails тЖТ fail closed, no fallback to local
- Redis release/renew are owner-checked
- renew is best-effort for long-running turns and logs renew failure/error

**Validation run:**

ds4_description: Adds distributed ACP session locking with Redis support and fail-closed behavior to prevent concurrent ACP execution across workers. Introduces SessionLockManager abstraction (local/Redis/fail-closed variants) wired into the ACP dispatch flow, with owner-checked acquire/release/renew semantics. The lock is enforced at the ACP dispatch boundary to skip concurrent runs with acp_execution_locked.

Redis locking implementation is new and may have edge cases not yet tested

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #71930: Mattermost plugin drops post_edited events тАФ @mentions added via edit do not trigger agent wake
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 71930
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/71930
- Title: Mattermost plugin drops post_edited events тАФ @mentions added via edit do not trigger agent wake
- State: OPEN
- Author: robbyproc87
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:source-repro, clawsweeper:linked-pr-open, impact:message-loss, issue-rating: ЁЯжЮ diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
### Summary

The Mattermost WebSocket plugin subscribes to `posted` events only. `post_edited` events are discarded before mention detection runs, so an @mention added by editing a message never wakes the tagged agent. The drop is silent тАФ no error, no log entry.

### Reproduction (confirmed 2026-04-25)

1. Post a message in a Mattermost channel with no @mention тЖТ agent correctly does not wake
2. Edit that message to add `@agentname` тЖТ agent does **not** wake (bug)
3. Post a fresh follow-up with `@agentname` in the original send тЖТ agent wakes correctly

### Root Cause

Two hard guards in `channel.runtime-Ci-PEA8h.js` reject any non-`posted` event:

**Line 590 тАФ `parsePostedPayload()`:**
```js
function parsePostedPayload(payload) {
  if (payload.event !== "posted") return null;
  // ...
}
```

**Line 723 тАФ WebSocket message handler:**
```js
if (payload.event !== "posted") return;
const parsed = parsePostedPayload(payload);
```

`post_edited` events from Mattermost'''s WebSocket API hit both guards and return early. Mention detection, wake-up, and session routing are never reached.

### Expected Behavior

When a message is edited to add an @mention, OpenClaw should evaluate the updated message body and fire the same mention/wake flow as if the @mention had been in the original post. Or at minimum, emit a warning log when a `post_edited` event contains an @mention that would have triggered wake.

### Actual Behavior

The `post_edited` event is silently dropped. The agent is never woken.

### OpenClaw Version

`2026.4.20`

### Workaround

Until fixed: @mentions must be included in the original `posted` message. If a tag is missed, send a fresh follow-up тАФ do not edit the original.

### Notes

This also affects any future use of Mattermost'''s edit-to-correct-mention UX pattern. Given that OpenClaw'''s own agents edit messages in-flight (e.g., "let me check memory" тЖТ edited to final tagged response), there is a real risk of agents tagging each other via edit and silently losing the wake event.

The fix likely involves adding a `post_edited` branch to the WebSocket handler that re-parses the updated post body and feeds it through the existing mention gate logic. Deduplication (don'''t re-wake for a non-mention edit) would be the main complexity.
```

Comments/context:
```markdown

**Workflow note:** Future ClawSweeper reviews update this same comment in place.

- PR/issue authors and users with repository write access can comment `@clawsweeper re-review` or `@clawsweeper re-run` on an open PR or issue to request a fresh review only.
- Maintainers can also comment `@clawsweeper review` to request a fresh review only.
- Fresh-review commands do not start repair, autofix, rebase, CI repair, or automerge.
- Maintainer-only repair and merge flows require explicit commands such as `@clawsweeper autofix`, `@clawsweeper automerge`, `@clawsweeper fix ci`, or `@clawsweeper address review`.
- Maintainers can comment `@clawsweeper explain` to ask for more context, or `@clawsweeper stop` to stop active automation.

</details>

**Summary**
Keep open: current main and the latest release still drop Mattermost `post_edited` WebSocket events before `onPosted`, so an edit-added mention cannot r

[comments/context truncated after 1500 chars]
```
ds4_description: The Mattermost plugin silently drops post_edited WebSocket events, so @mentions added via message edit never trigger agent wake. Root cause: two hard guards in the WebSocket handler reject any non-`posted` event before mention detection runs.
ds4_caveats: Fix complexity around deduplication is acknowledged but not analyzed here.

github_context:

- State: MERGED
- Author: extrasmall0
- Labels: cli, size: XS, proof: supplied, proof: sufficient, P2, rating: ЁЯРЪ platinum hermit, status: ЁЯСА ready for maintainer look
- Changed file count available to wrapper: 2
- Changed files: src/cli/acp-cli.option-collisions.test.ts, src/cli/acp-cli.ts

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

- Let Commander own the negated option default for --no-prefix-cwd.
- Read the positive prefixCwd option that Commander actually populates.
- Add ACP CLI coverage for both the explicit opt-out and the default behavior.

Closes #83901.

## Verification

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/acp-cli.option-collisions.test.ts src/acp/translator.prompt-prefix.test.ts
- Crabbox AWS cbx_1689d0ad78e9, run run_a406418db6fe: regression failed before the source patch, then the focused ACP CLI and translator tests passed after the patch (15/15).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: openclaw acp --no-prefix-cwd now forwards prefixCwd=false into the ACP bridge so prompts are not prefixed with [Working directory: ...]; the default path still forwards prefixCwd=true.
Real environment tested: Fresh checkout of this PR branch after pnpm install and pnpm build, using a live ACP translator harness from the checkout; maintainer follow-up also verified focused behavior on AWS Crabbox Linux lease cbx_1689d0ad78e9.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Build the PR checkout, run the ACP translator harness once with the default prefixCwd path and once with the --no-prefix-cwd / prefixCwd=false path, then run node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/acp-cli.option-collisions.test.ts src/acp/translator.prompt-prefix.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose.
Evidence after fix: The live ACP translator harness printed this after-fix output:

    default prefixCwd=true
    [Working directory: ~/openclaw-test]

    hello
    ---
    with --no-prefix-cwd / prefixCwd=false
    hello

Observed result after fix: The default path still prefixes the working directory, and the --no-prefix-cwd path suppresses that prefix. Focused tests also passed with 15 passed; the same regression test failed before the source patch with prefixCwd received as true.
What was not tested: No long-running interactive ACP server was left running; the live proof used a bounded ACP translator harness and the maintainer proof used focused CLI/translator tests.

```

<summary>How this review workflow works</summary>

- ClawSweeper keeps one durable marker-backed review comment per issue or PR.
- Re-runs edit this comment so the latest verdict, findings, and automation markers stay together instead of adding duplicate bot comments.
- A fresh review can be triggered by eligible `@clawsweeper re-review` comments, exact-item GitHub events, scheduled/background review runs, or manual workflow dispatch.
- PR/issue authors and users with repository write access can comment `@clawsweeper re-review` or `@clawsweeper re-run` on an open PR or issue to request a fresh review only.
- Maintainers can also comment `@clawsweeper review` to request a fresh review only.
- Fresh-review commands do not start repair, autofix, rebase, CI repair, or automerge.
- Maintainer-only repair and merge flows require explicit commands such as `@clawsweeper autofix`, `@clawsweeper automerge`, `@clawsweeper fix ci`, or `@clawsweeper address review`.
- Maintainers can comment `@clawsweeper explain` to ask for more context, or `@clawsweeper stop` to stop active automation.

</details>

**Summary**
The PR updates ACP CLI parsing so `--no-prefix-cwd` forwards `prefixCwd: false` and adds focused CLI coverage for the opt-out and default paths.

ds4_description: Fixes --no-prefix-cwd option parsing in the ACP CLI so Commander owns the negated boolean default and the positive prefixCwd option is read correctly, forwarding prefixCwd=false when --no-prefix-cwd is passed.
title: fix(cli): honor --no-prefix-cwd in acp

per_label_rationale.acp: PR explicitly fixes the `openclaw acp` CLI path so `--no-prefix-cwd` is forwarded into the ACP bridge.

excluded_label_rationale.api_surface: No HTTP/API endpoint or request/response contract changes; this is a CLI option parsing fix for ACP.
excluded_label_rationale.tests_ci: Tests were added, but only as coverage for the ACP CLI behavior, not a CI/test infrastructure change.

ambiguity.why_not_hard: The title, files, and body all directly identify ACP CLI behavior as the central change.

review_decision.notes: User reviewed and said this row is correct.
